programmes.show_description: In the Guise of Death: Compelling crime-cracking with the inspector and his down-to-earth lady chum. A horse trainer is found hanged in his stable, but Lynley doubts it was suicide.
The Female of the Species: Robert Hardy stars in the veterinary drama ideal with a cup of tea and a biscuit. Calum becomes more enthusiastic when he meets the new veterinary student.
Death n' Bass: Following Brokenwood's first drum 'n' bass festival, the van belonging to the band Starscraper suddenly explodes.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Food for Thought: Chigwell-set comedy featuring the co-habiting sisters and their oversexed neighbour. Garth causes a stir when he says he doesn't want to work in the city.
Blowing It All Away: PC Rickman becomes increasingly friendly with a drug user and soon comes under suspicion of using drugs herself.
New. Need to Know: An ex-girlfriend of Nick's turns up at the Mill, eager to turn the clock back. Vivien has a disastrous driving lesson when Eva comes along for the ride.
New. Walking Wounded: Daniel and Archie are obliged to work together on the case of a man forced to revisit his troubled past, and Davey flirts with Michelle in front of Ruth.
Inside Out: Diane brings Jack to work with her and is warned that she must choose between motherhood and her career. Mubbs tries to make amends with Rosie.
Season of Goodwill: Kirsty makes an important decision about her future after Warren is admitted to the ED. Yuki helps Lenny in his desperate search to find a donor match for his sister.
Blowing It All Away: PC Rickman becomes increasingly friendly with a drug user and soon comes under suspicion of using drugs herself.
New. Billie is determined to serve his country, but furious Carol has other ideas. Syed and Amira's windfall moves them away from Zainab, and Leon shows Zsa Zsa his romantic side.
New. Billie will not be budged on his plans to fight for Britain. Meanwhile, Amira unwittingly torments Zainab, and Danny plays Ronnie and Roxy off against each other.
The Jackpot: Vintage stuff from TV's golden age, with Christopher Timothy as the resourceful vet. James is challenged to a race, and a retired sheepdog takes up chasing cars.
Jan has a confrontation with Ken over his plans to buy into her fashion business. Meanwhile, Charles and Gerald prepare for their trial.
Die We Must: A missing manuscript case takes a dramatic turn when Frank and Lu find themselves trapped in a famous author's house, where no one is what they seem.
Love on the Run: More Essex comedy from the Chigwell sisters. Darryl has been taken to hospital with appendicitis and plans to escape and spend the night with his wife.
Inventor of the 40-foot Ferret: The sitcom's first series, with the loveable fellows enjoying a second childhood in Yorkshire. Clegg and Blamire encourage Compo's spirituality.
Pate and Chips: Classic comic adventures from the early days when Michael Bates was one of the naughty silvered trio. The chums go to a stately home, with predictable consequences!
Spring Fever: Compo makes an effort by dressing up smartly as he attempts to woo a lady. So he might take the ferrets out of his trousers... fun in the early days of the sitcom.

programmes.show_name: PD James: Death in Holy Orders
programmes.show_description: Atmospheric feature-length murder-mystery thriller, based on the PD James book. Adam Dalgliesh investigates a death and cover-up at a theological college he knows well.
Buried Treasure: Alun Armstrong and James Bolam star in the hugely popular crime caper. When Brian's dog finds a body on a common, it prompts two people to confess to murder.
The Seed of Cunning: Can't-tear-your-eyes-away murder mystery with the unlikely detective duo. Lynley and Havers investigate death and conspiracy at the House of Lords.
The Jackpot: Vintage stuff from TV's golden age, with Christopher Timothy as the resourceful vet. James is challenged to a race, and a retired sheepdog takes up chasing cars.
Spark to a Flame: Lynley Foster moved to a community of beach cottages at Pteke Point in search of peace and quiet, but that dream is dashed by her disagreeable neighbour, Wyatt.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Commitments: Sitcom set in Chigwell, Essex - a mine of comedy! Sharon's husband wants to dump her for another woman and take the cafe away from her.
All Change: Sun Hill's CID join the uniform beat on the biggest night of the century - the eve of the new millennium.
New. Love and War: George helps a bickering couple realise they need each other when they are both struck down by serious illness.
New. No. 786: Michelle invites Ruth out and Jimmi gets caught up in a difficult situation on a bus when passengers become suspicious of a Muslim boy who's pacing and muttering prayers.
While the Cat's Away: A flamboyant plastic surgeon shows up to treat a private patient, earning Will's immediate disapproval. Meanwhile, Ric proposes a spot of drunken male bonding.
Winter Wonderland: The precarious state of Ruth's marriage threatens to ruin her Christmas. Mads and Noel throw a party in the ED. Kirsty struggles to put aside her feelings for Adam.
All Change: Sun Hill's CID join the uniform beat on the biggest night of the century - the eve of the new millennium.
New. Christian resolves to sort things out with Syed, as Carol begins to shift on the idea of Billie the soldier. Danny and Glenda plot to take the Mitchells for all they've got.
New. Jack comes out of his coma to find his life will never be the same again. Amira enlists Christian's help to save her marriage, and the mysteries of the fairer sex baffle Leon.
Two of a Kind: Fun with the vets on patrol in the gorgeous 1930s Yorkshire countryside. TV arrives in the Dales, and Siegfried treats a dog with a weak heart.
Charles vows to take revenge on Ken for trying to ruin him. Meanwhile, Jan and James successfully exhibit their clothing at a Maltese fashion show.
Some Cupid Kills: Frank and Lu investigate a suspicious death among a team of actors who offer Shakespeare for hire for any occasion.
Old Friends: Sitcom with those Essex sisters. Tracey invites an old schoolfriend to stay in the guest room. As the pair relive their past adventures, Sharon begins to feel deserted.
The New Mobile Trio: A rust-bucket is about to find some new owners in another episode from the first series of the world's longest-running sitcom. The boys decide they need a car.
Hail Smiling Morn, or Thereabouts: Laughs with the outrageous old codgers. The gang have some fun with a new camera, tracking down things to photograph. Not Nora's stockings?
Forked Lightning: Laughs with the old lads roaming around the beautiful Yorkshire countryside. There are problems with a bicycle as Sid tries to avoid Ivy. Who can blame him?
An Appetite for Murder: Part One: When a popular but tempestuous celebrity chef is murdered during his cookbook launch, there's no shortage of suspects for Judith, Suzie and Becks.
An Appetite for Murder: Part Two: As police investigate the chef's death, a new development makes them rethink everything they have learned so far. Guest starring Susan Lynch.
Father's Pride: James Bolam and Dennis Waterman (who does indeed sing the theme tune) star in the great mystery series. The team probe a murder in a photographer's shop.
